note: Human-validated compliance metadata. Derived from official ECC-2:2024 document and NCA guidance. Does not contain regulatory text.

controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: full-time Saudi cybersecurity professional requirement extended to all cybersecurity positions, not just head and critical roles.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Risk assessment must be conducted at each triggering event listed in subcontrols.
Applies when nationally approved international agreements with cybersecurity requirements exist.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified and renumbered in ECC-2: previous 1-7-1 (comply with national cybersecurity laws) deleted; previous 1-7-2 renumbered to this control.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Audit must be conducted by parties independent from the cybersecurity department, per Generally Accepted Auditing Standards (GAAS).
Applies to cybersecurity positions and technical positions with critical or privileged powers.

controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: renamed to single-factor authentication based on username and password.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Applies to remote access and privileged accounts. MFA scope expanded in ECC-2 to include cloud management consoles and infrastructure.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: expanded from MFA for remote access to include impact assessment basis. Scope includes cloud consoles and infrastructure.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Applies to remote and webmail access. Scope expanded in ECC-2 to require impact assessment approach.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: authentication scope broadened; impact assessment basis added for webmail access.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Previous ECC-1 version required SPF only. ECC-2 adds DKIM and DMARC.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: DKIM and DMARC added to previous SPF-only email domain validation requirement.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: New subcontrol added in ECC-2. No ECC-1 equivalent.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: New in ECC-2: DDoS protection requirement consolidated into a dedicated subcontrol.
Modified in ECC-2: added 'based on its classification level' to implementation requirement.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: ECC-1 control 2-7-3 with privacy subcontrols was deleted. This is the renumbered review control. Privacy requirements transferred to NDMO.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Control renumbered in ECC-2: old 2-7-3 (privacy subcontrols) deleted; privacy requirements moved to Saudi Data and AI Authority (NDMO).

controls_metadata.applicability_note: ECC-2 explicitly references NCA National Cryptographic Standards publication.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: now explicitly requires compliance with NCA National Cryptographic Standards and prohibits deprecated algorithms.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Scope includes all externally provided services: infrastructure, websites, web apps, mobile apps, email, and remote access.
Monitoring must be continuous, not periodic.
Minimum retention period is 12 months per official ECC-2 text.
Reporting to NCA is mandatory per High Order No. 37140 dated 14/08/1438H.
Sharing with NCA includes incident notifications, threat intelligence, penetration indicators, and incident reports.
Covers data centers, disaster recovery centers, critical processing facilities, security surveillance centers, network connection rooms, and technical device areas.
Applies to physical assets including paper documents and storage media.
ECC-2 broadened authentication approach to include impact assessment basis, covering privileged remote administration scenarios.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: expanded from MFA requirement to full user authentication guidance based on impact assessment of authentication failure.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Applies to third-party contracts where impairment may affect the entity's data or services.
Applies specifically to IT outsourcing, cybersecurity outsourcing, and managed services contracts.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: scope clarified to explicitly cover IT outsourcing, cybersecurity outsourcing, and managed services.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Cybersecurity managed service centers using remote access must be fully located in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Applies to entities that use cloud computing or hosting services.
Applies to entities that use cloud computing or hosting services.
Applies in addition to applicable controls from Domains 1, 2, 3, and Subdomain 4-1. ECC-1 subcontrol 4-2-3-3 (data localization) was deleted in ECC-2.
controls_metadata.change_from_ecc1: Modified in ECC-2: subcontrol 4-2-3-3 (entity's data must be hosted inside KSA) was deleted. Data localization requirements now governed by NDMO/Saudi Data and AI Authority.
Modified in ECC-2: focus shifted from pre-hosting classification to ongoing protection in accordance with classification level.

controls_metadata.applicability_note: Requires environment separation at the virtual server level.
Applies to entities that use cloud computing or hosting services.
